description: Registered node type list for the Chronos knowledge graph.

node_types.label: Person
node_types.description: An individual human being with a lifespan. Includes philosophers, rulers, scientists, artists, and any other figure whose life and work are tracked.
node_types.temporal_semantics: start/end are birth and death years
node_types.spatial_semantics: primary coordinates are birthplace or primary location of activity

node_types.label: Event
node_types.description: A discrete occurrence with a bounded time frame — a battle, a publication, a discovery, a natural disaster, a political transition.
node_types.temporal_semantics: start is the event's occurrence year; end marks its conclusion if prolonged
node_types.spatial_semantics: primary coordinates are the event's location

node_types.label: Period
node_types.description: An era, movement, dynasty, or age with a temporal span — Bronze Age, Hellenistic period, Abbasid Caliphate. Periods are background temporal containers.
node_types.temporal_semantics: start and end delimit the period

node_types.label: Place
node_types.description: A city, region, empire, or geographic feature. Places have their own temporal extent (a city can be founded and destroyed) and participate in the graph as first-class nodes.
node_types.temporal_semantics: start and end mark the place's historical existence or relevance
node_types.spatial_semantics: primary coordinates are the geographic location

node_types.label: Work
node_types.description: A text, artwork, monument, or other cultural product that is itself a node in the graph — independent of its author. Enables tracking of a text's influence trajectory.
node_types.temporal_semantics: start is the composition or creation year
node_types.spatial_semantics: primary coordinates are the place of composition

node_types.label: Concept
node_types.description: A named idea or paradigm with a traceable geographic and temporal trajectory — e.g. 'the Logos', 'karma', 'natural law'. Distinct from a school or tradition.
node_types.temporal_semantics: start marks first known articulation; end marks obsolescence if applicable
node_types.spatial_semantics: primary is the origin point; spatial_mode often 'diffuse'

node_types.label: Institution
node_types.description: A school, court, library, academy, religious body, or other organizational structure. Institutions are the vessels of transmission.
node_types.temporal_semantics: start is founding; end is dissolution or transformation
node_types.spatial_semantics: primary coordinates are the physical location

node_types.label: Technology
node_types.description: A technique or tool — writing, the stirrup, papermaking, the astrolabe — that spreads and transforms across cultures. Technologies have origin points and diffusion paths.
node_types.temporal_semantics: start is the approximate invention or first documentation date
node_types.spatial_semantics: primary is origin; active_regions track diffusion

node_types.label: Route
node_types.description: A trade, migration, or transmission path. Routes are spatial objects connecting two or more place nodes.
node_types.temporal_semantics: start and end mark the route's historical period of use
node_types.spatial_semantics: spatial_mode should be 'path'; primary may be a midpoint or anchor

node_types.label: Phenomenon
node_types.description: An environmental, climatic, epidemiological, or large-scale social event — a plague, a famine, a climate period, a financial crisis. Phenomena shape history as background conditions.
node_types.temporal_semantics: start and end delimit the phenomenon's duration

node_types.label: Dataset
node_types.description: A pointer to an external structured data file or database relevant to the graph — archaeological surveys, demographic data, climate proxies.
node_types.temporal_semantics: start and end indicate the temporal coverage of the dataset
